outlook.headline: High-NA EUV is progressing, but adoption remains uneven
outlook.summary: High-NA EUV is clearly moving forward, with ASML saying customers are testing it on real product and seeing strong readiness across multiple nodes. The upside is concentrated in the most advanced-node and enabling-materials names like Intel, Micron, and Veeco, while several other companies remain neutral and TSMC still flags EUV supply as a bottleneck, so breadth should improve only gradually over the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: Real-product testing and multi-node readiness
drivers.detail: ASML says High-NA is progressing well, with customers testing it on real product and showing strong readiness across multiple nodes.

drivers.label: Advanced-node ramps pulling more EUV content
drivers.detail: Intel points to higher EUV wafer mix and 18A growth, implying stronger near-term demand for High-NA-related tools.

drivers.label: Scaling efficiency benefits for future nodes
drivers.detail: Micron says more advanced EUV should improve efficiency and enable tighter future node scaling.

drivers.label: Enabling materials demand improving
drivers.detail: Veeco sees rising High-NA adoption supporting demand for EUV mask blanks and other enabling materials.

drivers.label: Supply bottlenecks still limit breadth
drivers.detail: TSMC cites EUV supply as an issue, and the topic’s mention trend is slightly below average, suggesting adoption remains constrained.

risks: High-NA stays at the testing stage and fails to convert into broader volume orders.
Persistent EUV supply constraints could delay shipments and slow adoption timing.
Neutral coverage remains dominant outside the key bullish names, limiting breadth despite positive leader commentary.
